title: Application Insights
html:
Application Insights

Autoinstrumentation in Application Insights refers to the automatic collection of telemetry data from various components of an application without requiring manual code changes or instrumentation. Application Insights is a monitoring and analytics service provided by Microsoft Azure that helps developers track the performance, usage, and availability of their applications.

With autoinstrumentation, Application Insights can automatically collect telemetry data from different layers and components of an application, including web servers, web frameworks, databases, and client-side code. This eliminates the need for developers to manually add code snippets or instrumentation libraries to their application code to collect telemetry.

Key features of auto-instrumentation in Application Insights include:

1. Automatic Dependency Tracking: Application Insights automatically tracks dependencies between different components of an application, such as outgoing HTTP requests, database queries, and external service calls. This provides insights into the performance and reliability of external dependencies and helps identify bottlenecks or issues impacting application performance.

2. Automatic Performance Monitoring: Autoinstrumentation captures performance metrics such as response times, throughput, and error rates for different components of an application without requiring developers to add custom instrumentation code. This allows developers to monitor the performance of their applications in real-time and identify performance optimizations or areas for improvement.

3. Automatic Error and Exception Tracking: Application Insights automatically captures exceptions, errors, and stack traces from application code, allowing developers to identify and diagnose issues quickly. Autoinstrumentation tracks exceptions across different layers of an application, including server-side code, client-side JavaScript, and background tasks, providing comprehensive error monitoring and troubleshooting capabilities.

4. Automatic User and Session Tracking: Autoinstrumentation tracks user interactions and session data to provide insights into user behavior, engagement, and retention. This includes tracking user sessions, page views, user actions, and conversion events without requiring developers to add custom tracking code.

5. Integration with Azure Services: Application Insights seamlessly integrates with other Azure services such as Azure Monitor, Azure DevOps, and Azure Functions, allowing developers to leverage autoinstrumentation capabilities within their existing Azure workflows and environments.

Overall, autoinstrumentation in Application Insights simplifies the process of monitoring and troubleshooting applications by automatically collecting telemetry data from various components and layers of an application, enabling developers to gain insights into application performance, usage, and reliability without the need for manual instrumentation.
